ingredients: 500 g very fresh fish fillet (e.g., tanigue/kingfish), cut into 1 to 1.5 cm cubes
1/2 to 3/4 cup cane or coconut vinegar, divided
Juice of 10 to 15 calamansi (or lime/lemon to taste)
Salt and black pepper, to taste
steps: Rinse fish quickly in cold lightly salted water, then drain very well and keep chilled.
Toss fish with a pinch of salt and about half the vinegar. Let stand 5 to 10 minutes, then drain off the first vinegar.
Add remaining vinegar, calamansi juice, ginger, onion and chilies; toss gently to coat.
Season with salt, pepper and a touch of sugar if desired. Chill another 5 to 10 minutes.
Fold in coconut cream just before serving if using, and serve immediately while cold.
tips: Use only very fresh fish and keep everything cold; do not marinate too long or the texture can become chalky.
